items.title: President vs. Pope: How feud with Leo could hurt Trump
items.summary: US President Donald Trump has feuded with Pope Leo XIV over the Iran war – setting off an unholy row that could have serious political implications for the Republican leader at home. Trump has drawn barbs even from some allies over the attacks on the US-born pontiff, who has criticised the Trump administration over its immigration crackdown, the intervention in Venezuela and the Iran war. He's wrong," the 79-year-old Trump told reporters at the White House on Monday.

items.title: KTMB offers 30pct weekday train fare discount from April to October
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Keretapi Tanah Melayu Bhd (KTMB) is offering a 30 per cent discount on train tickets across all its sectors from tomorrow until April 20. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said that, in addition to Electric Train Service (ETS) tickets, the discount for travel between April 15 and Oct 14 will also apply to passengers of the Ekspres Rakyat Timuran (ERT) for the JB Sentral–Tumpat–JB Sentral route. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Founder of China's Evergrande pleads guilty to fraud: court
items.summary: BEIJING: The founder of Chinese property giant Evergrande has pleaded guilty to charges of fraud and bribery, a court said on Tuesday, in the latest blow for what was once the country's leading developer. A public hearing was held Monday and Tuesday on a case against Xu for "illegally absorbing public deposits, fundraising fraud, illegally issuing loans, illegally using funds, fraudulently issuing securities, disclosing important information in violation of regulations, embezzlement and (corporate) bribery", a court statement said. "Xu Jiayin pleaded guilty and expressed remorse in court," the statement from the Shenzhen Intermediate People's Court in southern Guangdong province said, without elaborating further.

items.title: Bukit Aman internal security unit roped into Port Klang e-waste task force
items.summary: PORT KLANG: The Port Klang Joint Task Force on e-waste has agreed to bring in the police's Internal Security and Public Order Department to strengthen ongoing enforcement operations. Port Klang MCBA commander Datuk Nik Ezanee Mohd Faisal said the decision was reached following discussions with the department's deputy director Datuk Mohamad Suzrin Mohamad Rodhi, as well as other members of the joint task force. "The decision to add them to the task force was unanimous, and they will be instrumental in our operations," he told reporters here today.

items.title: Police raided Pavilion Residence unit after CCTV showed govt vehicles moving boxes, bags, High Court told
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 14 — A former senior police officer told the High Court today that the order to raid an unoccupied unit at Pavilion Residences in May 2018 was prompted by closed-circuit television (CCTV) footage showing several boxes and bags being transported in vehicles bearing the official markings of the Prime Minister’s Department, just days after the 14th General Election. Former Bukit Aman Commercial Crime Investigation Department director Datuk Seri Amar Singh said he was informed by a subordinate that CCTV footage from Tower A of Pavilion Residences, dated May 11, 2018, showed several individuals moving a trolley laden with orange-coloured boxes into a unit on Level 45. He also said several trips were made transporting the bags and boxes into the unit.

items.title: Two victims in Lebuh King law firm fire died of smoke inhalation
items.summary: Northeast district deputy police chief Superintendent Lee Swee Sake said the victims comprised renowned veteran lawyer Datuk San Markan Ganapathi, 88, and Chandra Binwani Jiwatram, 60, a clerk at the firm. "Both victims are confirmed to have died from asphyxia due to smoke inhalation," he said, adding that the post-mortem findings were completed following forensic examinations. Yesterday, Penang Fire and Rescue Department director Mohamad Shoki Hamzah said the two victims who perished each suffered 30 per cent burns .

items.title: PM orders swift response after religious school fire
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has instructed relevant agencies to expedite immediate action following a fire at SMU(A) Darul Ulum Muhammadiah in Kelantan. He said the incident was a major test for the school community and stressed that urgent steps must be taken to ensure safety and recovery efforts are carried out swiftly. He said student welfare remains a key priority for the government.

items.title: US seeking 20-year pause of Iran uranium enrichment
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The United States sought a 20-year suspension of Iran's uranium enrichment programme as part of a deal to end the war, according to media reports Monday, after Washington and Teheran failed to come to an agreement. Vice President JD Vance left negotiations with Iran over the weekend empty-handed, with sticking points including freeing up the Strait of Hormuz and Iran's nuclear programme. Iran in turn proposed to suspend its nuclear activity for five years, according to The New York Times.

items.title: Most Malaysians happy at work, but stress remains concern
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Most Malaysian workers say they are happy at work, but rising stress and burnout suggest workplace wellbeing remains more fragile than headline figures indicate. According to Jobstreet by Seek's latest Workplace Happiness Index, 70 per cent of workers report being somewhat or extremely happy in their jobs. A similar proportion say they feel valued at work, while 68 per cent find their work fulfilling.

items.title: Gen Z workers report lower happiness, higher stress
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Gen Z workers in Malaysia report lower workplace happiness and higher stress levels compared with older generations, highlighting growing pressures among early-career employees. According to Jobstreet by Seek's Workplace Happiness Index, Gen Z employees are more likely to experience stress as they navigate early career challenges, skill development and the need to prove themselves. They are also more likely to say that reducing stress would significantly improve their happiness, at 40 per cent, compared with 35 per cent for millennials and 27 per cent for Gen X, the report said.

items.title: Xi meets Spain PM in China as Madrid seeks stronger ties
items.summary: BEIJING: China's President Xi Jinping met Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez in Beijing on Tuesday, according to Chinese state media, with the visit centred on strengthening economic ties. "On the morning of April 14, President Xi Jinping met with Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez, who is on an official visit to China, at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing," state broadcaster CCTV reported. The Spanish prime minister is on his fourth visit to China in four years and follows a steady flow of Western leaders visiting Beijing in recent months as President Donald Trump's tariffs and unpredictable foreign policy have rattled the US's traditional allies.

items.title: Two killed as chicken-laden lorry overturns on WCE near Sitiawan
items.summary: BERUAS: Two men were killed after a lorry carrying chickens skidded and overturned at Km194.7 of the West Coast Expressway (WCE) near Sitiawan today. Perak Fire and Rescue Department operations assistant director Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said the department received a distress call at 10.11am, and a team of firefighters from Sitiawan rushed to the scene. "Two male victims were thrown out of the vehicle and became trapped beside the lorry," he said.

items.title: Mideast war revs up electric car demand in Asia
items.summary: HANOI: Electric vehicle sales have jumped in Southeast Asia as cost-conscious buyers have poured into dealerships looking to dodge the fuel price spikes driven by the Middle East war. Yet the energy crisis has been a windfall for Vietnam's leading electric vehicle maker Vinfast as well as Chinese manufacturers. Dao Thi Hue, also at the showroom, was looking to go electric too.

items.title: '10pct return in 30 minutes' lure leaves man RM427,000 poorer
items.summary: The 44-year-old private sector employee was lured by promises of 10 per cent returns within 30 minutes of investment through the scheme. Pahang police chief Datuk Seri Yahaya Othman said the victim was contacted by an unknown individual via WhatsApp on Dec 17 last year, inviting him to invest in a "Growth Investment" scheme. "The victim was attracted to the scheme, which promised a 10 per cent return within 30 minutes.

items.title: No medications were administered to Zara on the way to hospital
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: No medications, including the anti-epileptic drug, phenytoin, were given to the late Zara Qairina Mahathir before she arrived at the Queen Elizabeth Hospital (QEH), the coroner's court was told. Medical assistant Mohd Faiszal Md Saimi from Kinarut Health Clinic, testified that no drugs were administered during the initial emergency treatment until his team handed Zara Qairina over to personnel at QEH. He also denied administering phenytoin, which is an anti-epileptic drug.

items.title: PKR slams Trump's retort to Pope's plea for peace
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: PKR's International Affairs Bureau has expressed its solidarity with Pope Leo XIV, commending his principled stand against rising military tensions involving the United States and Iran. The bureau's chairman, Dr Maszlee Malik, praised the Pope's call for restraint and the sanctity of human life amid the beating of the drums of war. "Keadilan views with contempt the retort issued by US President Donald Trump against the leader of the Catholic Church.

items.title: My50 stays: Govt keeps subsidised transport passes for Klang Valley commuters
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 14 — The government will continue its subsidised public transport pass schemes, including the widely used My50 programme, as part of efforts to ease the impact of rising fuel costs on urban commuters. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the initiative forms part of the broader rail usage campaign to address rising fuel costs. “The My50 pass allows unlimited travel on rail and bus services in the Klang Valley for RM50 a month — about 67 per cent lower than its actual value,” Loke told a press conference here today.

items.title: MCBA logs 231 enforcement cases involving RM36.2mil in goods
items.summary: PORT KLANG: The Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA) has taken enforcement action on 231 containers between January and March this year, involving goods valued at RM36.2 million. Port Klang MCBA commander Datuk Nik Ezanee Mohd Faisal said the cases stemmed from investigations conducted by multiple agencies, including Maqis, the Health Ministry and the Customs Department. "Overall, enforcement actions taken by MCBA from January to March involved 231 containers, with a total value of RM36,243,214.11," he said at a press conference today.

items.title: Putrajaya pushes rail relief plan with discounted KTMB fares and monthly passes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 14 — The government today introduced a rail-focused relief package offering discounted fares and new monthly pass options in an effort to cushion Malaysians from rising commuting costs linked to fuel prices. Transport Minister Anthony Loke Siew Fook said the initiative is aimed at encouraging greater use of public transport, particularly rail, while improving affordability for daily commuters. “The discount applies for travel from Monday to Thursday, excluding school holidays and public holidays, in a move aimed at managing demand while promoting more consistent ridership.

items.title: Over 400,000 ANPR transactions recorded since January, says Nanta
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: More than 400,000 transactions have been recorded through the Automated Number Plate Recognition (ANPR) system since its pilot rollout in January. The pilot project covers the Hutan Kampung–Sungai Dua stretch, involving nine toll plazas, namely, Hutan Kampung, Alor Star Utara, Alor Star Selatan, Pendang, Gurun, Sungai Petani Utara, Sungai Petani Selatan, Bertam and Sungai Dua. Nanta said the ANPR system is currently operational at nine toll plazas nationwide, covering 78 lanes.

items.title: MCBA seizes RM1 million worth of frozen meat over falsified documents
items.summary: PORT KLANG: The Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA) has seized seven containers of frozen meat worth more than RM1 million following the discovery of suspected falsified import documents. Port Klang MCBA commander Datuk Nik Ezanee Mohd Faisal said the seizure at North Port on April 9 was based on a public tip-off. The operation involved six containers of frozen chicken and one container of frozen duck, weighing a total of 187,800kg.

items.title: First batch of 15 e-waste containers shipped out of Malaysia
items.summary: PORT KLANG: Malaysian authorities have shipped out the first batch of 15 containers of illegal electronic waste (e-waste), marking a key milestone in ongoing enforcement efforts at Port Klang. Port Klang Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA) commander Datuk Nik Ezanee Mohd Faisal said the containers were transported via Westports at 8am today as part of coordinated operations under Op Green Shield. "Alhamdulillah, today 15 e-waste containers have been shipped out.

items.title: Mida Singapore sees growing investment into Johor
items.summary: SINGAPORE: Johor continues to see a good traction of investment coming from Singapore, providing a seamless journey for investors looking to expand their businesses in the southern state, said the Malaysian Investment Development Authority (Mida). MIDA Singapore director Vinothan Tulisinathzan said that among the major industries attracting investment from Singapore are the digital economy, semiconductor, and food and beverages (F&B) businesses. He noted that investment opportunities in the digital economy within Johor fell into two main sections -- digital technology providers and digital infrastructure providers.

items.title: Medicine supply stable until June, public told to avoid stockpiling
items.summary: Health Ministry Pharmacy Practice and Development Division senior principal assistant director Muhammad Md Zin said stocks at government healthcare facilities are continuously monitored to ensure uninterrupted supply. He cautioned that excessive stockpiling was impractical as medicines have expiry dates and require specific storage conditions. It is best to manage medication wisely, avoid bulk purchases and seek advice from healthcare professionals," he said.

items.title: #NSTviral: Netizens back public lesson as illegally parked car in Kajang gets 'rubbish treatment'
items.summary: KAJANG: A Facebook post showing a car obstructing a shop lot road in Sepakat Indah 2 has gone viral, with many netizens expressing support with the person who left rubbish on the vehicle. The obstruction appeared to disrupt traffic flow, forcing other road users to carefully manoeuvre past the car. According to the caption accompanying the post, the vehicle owner had allegedly left the car in the middle of the road while dining nearby, blocking access for others.

items.title: MBSB IB sees Brent, CPO holding above support
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: MBSB Investment Bank Bhd expects benchmark Brent crude oil and crude palm oil prices to remain above support levels and potentially re-test resistance at US$114 per barrel and RM4,890 per tonne, respectively, in the near term. RHB raised its average Brent crude oil forecast for 2026 to US$82.50 per barrel from US$62 previously, and introduced a 2027 forecast of US$72 per barrel. "We revise our Brent assumptions for 2026 and 2027 to US$90 per barrel and US$75 per barrel, from US$70 and US$65 previously," it said.

items.title: Food poisoning row heads to court as MCMC seeks RM1.85m from Sunway Lagoon
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 14 — The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission’s (MCMC) RM1.85 million lawsuit against Sunway Lagoon Sdn Bhd has moved a step closer to trial following recent court filings. The suit, first filed at the High Court in Shah Alam on January 2, is now scheduled for case management on April 29 before Justice Anita Harun, after the MCMC submitted new documents last week, news portal Free Malaysia Today reported. The suit stems from an alleged food poisoning incident during an MCMC-organised family day event held at the theme park in late 2025.

items.title: PKR bureau backs Pope Leo’s anti-war stance, denounces Trump’s ‘insolent’ retort
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 14 — The PKR International Affairs Bureau has declared its solidarity with Pope Leo XIV, commending his principled stand against the threat of war between the United States and Iran. Bureau chairman Maszlee Malik praised the Pope’s call for peace as the embodiment of moral courage while condemning US President Donald Trump’s “militarism” as a violation of international law. Trump publicly attacked Pope Leo in response to the pontiff’s criticism of the US-Israel war in Iran as well as Trump’s hard‑line immigration and security policies.

items.title: Phillip Capital initiates Keyfield with 'Buy', TP RM1.70
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Phillip Research Sdn Bhd (Phillip Capital) has initiated coverage on Keyfield International Bhd with a "Buy" call and a target price of RM1.70, citing strong earnings growth over the next three years. Keyfield recently expanded its fleet with three vessels, namely Keyfield Gratitude, Keyfield Blessing and Keyfield Harmony, which are expected to contribute a full year of earnings in 2026. The firm also highlighted Keyfield's expansion into the Middle East following its 65 per cent acquisition of Keyfield Biham.

items.title: Bus driver in viral video is married, say police [WATCH]
items.summary: JASIN: Police have confirmed that the express bus driver seen in a viral video driving with a woman seated on his lap is married. District police chief Superintendent Lee Robert said the 36-year-old man is believed to reside in Johor, while the identity of the woman has yet to be established. He said police have not, at this stage, obtained further information regarding the relationship between the driver and the woman involved in the incident.

items.title: #NSTviral: Late-night delivery turns touching as customer gives duit raya to disabled rider [WATCH]
items.summary: MACHANG: A late-night food delivery has captured hearts online after a customer shared a brief but meaningful encounter with a disabled rider. The woman said the man, known on TikTok as @zaik.grabrider94, was someone who often delivered her orders, describing him as hardworking and determined despite his condition. @mcteqmierah97 MasyaAllah semoga abang grab ni dikurniakan rezeki yang tak henti2 amin #ibuscrubbywna #founderibuscrubwna original sound - romiez "May you be blessed with abundant sustenance, a long life, good health, and always be under Gods protection," wrote @ Efa Medina.

items.title: Johari pledges RM50,000 to Southeast Asia's largest gurdwara
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Investment, Trade and Industry Minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani has pledged RM50,000 to the Guru Nanak Darbar Tatt Khalsa Diwan, Southeast Asia's largest gurdwara. Johari made the announcement during the Vaisakhi Day celebration, attended by members of the Sikh community. He also said the government would look into a request to install solar energy systems at the gurdwara to help reduce electricity costs.

items.title: Sultan Nazrin warns of rising climate risks, urges shift in environmental mindset
items.summary: IPOH: Environmental management can no longer be treated as a secondary concern in an era of accelerating climate change, as its impacts are already being felt across the globe, warned the Sultan of Perak, Sultan Nazrin Muizzuddin Shah. Sultan Nazrin said extreme weather events and climate-related disasters have increasingly caused widespread destruction to property, disrupted livelihoods, and forced population displacement in many countries. "Extreme weather events and climate-related disasters have caused widespread damage to property and forced population displacement.

items.title: Govt to ensure 'projek rakyat' continue despite rising costs, global conflict
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government will ensure all people-oriented projects (projek rakyat) proceed despite the ongoing conflict abroad and rising costs, said Works Minister Datuk Seri Alexander Nanta Linggi. He said the ministry remained committed to safeguarding projects that directly benefit the rakyat, even as global uncertainties, including geopolitical conflicts and fuel price hikes, continue to impact the construction sector. Nanta said the ministry, through the Public Works Department (PWD), was exploring several mitigation measures to ensure projects could continue without disruption.

items.title: High Court orders review of fines for 34 men in Cheras weapons riot case
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 14 — The High Court yesterday ordered a revision hearing for 34 men previously fined for rioting with weapons at an entertainment outlet in Cheras. According to Free Malaysia Today , Justice Aslam Zainuddin said the court would examine the correctness, legality, and propriety of the sentences imposed by the magistrates’ court. Last Friday, the Magistrates’ Court fined all 34 men after they pleaded guilty to rioting at an entertainment centre in Cheras.

items.title: Another riot at KL entertainment centre, 13 arrested
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have arrested 13 men over a riot that broke out in front of an entertainment centre in Kepong. Sentul police chief Assistant Commissioner Basri Sagoni said the suspects, aged between 18 and 45, have been remanded until April 16. MORE TO COME © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: No need to turn your house into a pharmacy: MoH says medicine supplies stable, avoid hoarding
items.summary: Senior Principal Assistant Director of the Pharmacy Logistics Management Branch, Pharmacy Practice and Development Division of the Ministry of Health (MOH), Muhammad Md Zin, said medicine stocks at government healthcare facilities are continuously monitored to ensure uninterrupted supply to patients. He cautioned that excessive stockpiling is not practical, as medicines have expiry dates and require proper storage conditions. “Keeping excessive supplies increases the risk of using damaged or expired medicines, which can be harmful to health.

items.title: Putrajaya: Malaysia’s essential goods supply stable, no need for panic buying
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 14 — Consumers need not resort to panic buying despite the ongoing conflict in West Asia, as the supply of basic goods remains sufficient and well-controlled, according to the Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN). Its Kuala Lumpur chief enforcement officer, Mohd Shahran Mohd Arshad, said the ministry is continuously monitoring and supervising the prices and availability of goods throughout the period. “The KPDN Kuala Lumpur assures that the supply of essential daily necessities is adequate and under control.

items.title: Penang obtains approval for international logistics aeropark, earthworks underway
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 14 — Penang has obtained planning permission for the masterplan of the Penang International Logistics Aeropark (PILA). Penang chief minister Chow Kon Yeow said the project, a joint venture between Penang Development Corporation and Malaysia Airports Holdings Berhad (MAHB), is aimed at developing a new cargo terminal to strengthen the logistics ecosystem in the northern region. Chow said PILA has already secured leasing commitments from international logistics companies as well as local industry players.

items.title: Passenger who shot viral video of lap-sitting on moving bus says he acted to prevent danger
items.summary: KULAI, April 14 — A passenger on the express bus said he felt compelled to intervene after witnessing the driver allegedly carrying on inappropriate behaviour while the vehicle was in motion. The man, who only wished to be known as Siva, 40, told Harian Metro that he later lodged an online report after seeing a woman sitting on the driver’s lap while the bus was being driven. “I felt the act could lead to problems and compromise passenger safety.

items.title: Sacked bus driver and partner now wanted by police over viral lap-sitting stunt on Jasin highway
items.summary: JASIN, April 14 — Police are tracking down a male express bus driver and a woman believed to be his acquaintance to assist in investigations into a viral video showing a dangerous driving incident along the North-South Expressway in Jasin. Jasin police chief Supt Lee Robert said the eight-second clip, uploaded on Facebook, showed the woman sitting on the driver’s lap while the bus was in motion. “Preliminary investigations indicate the incident occurred on April 12 at about 7.30 pm near the southbound entrance of the Bemban Rest and Recreation (R&R) area.

items.title: Kelantan police: Teacher to face court soon over crash that killed schoolgirl outside surau
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 14 — A teacher suspected of fatally hitting a Form One student in a crash outside a school surau will be charged in court soon, police said today. Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said the 57-year-old woman, who was released on police bail this morning after her remand expired, will face charges once investigations are completed. The woman is suspected of hitting two students outside the surau of SMK Tanjung Mas on April 12, killing Nur Fatimatul Hawa Mohd Azaudin, 13, and seriously injuring another student.

items.title: Cops identify KL hit-and-run suspect as police inspector with schizophrenia, facing medical retirement
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 14 — The driver detained for allegedly being involved in a hit-and-run case involving a traffic policeman last Friday is a police inspector who suffers from a mental health condition, according to police. Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said the 40-year-old male suspect has been diagnosed with schizophrenia and is currently on special medical leave, as well as in the process of being retired on medical grounds upon the recommendation of the Medical Board. He said the suspect was arrested at 3.45 pm yesterday at Jalan Empat, Chan Sow Lin after personnel from the Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department tracked down his vehicle in the Jalan Cheras area through intelligence and closed-circuit television footage checks.

items.title: Perak Sultan warns leaders not to exploit West Asia conflict for political purposes, urges unity
items.summary: IPOH, April 14 —The Sultan of Perak, Sultan Nazrin Muizzuddin Shah, today reminded political leaders not to exploit the situation in West Asia for narrow partisan interests or to sensationalise the issue. Sultan Nazrin called on members of the State Legislative Assembly as well as community and business leaders to find common ground and work together in unity. “The transition to alternative energy must be carefully planned, expedited, and implemented with full commitment.

items.title: Public awareness on plastic waste still very low, says Perak Sultan
items.summary: IPOH, April 14 — Public awareness of the dangers posed by plastic waste remains low, despite ongoing intervention efforts, said the Sultan of Perak, Sultan Nazrin Muizzuddin Shah. Sultan Nazrin expressed concern over the worsening situation, calling for urgent and decisive action to address environmental degradation. “The threat of plastic waste is never-ending.

items.title: Baby girl found alive in Skudai surau car park, still with umbilical cord
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 14 — A newly born girl was found alive in a car park area of a surau in Taman Universiti, Skudai, here last Saturday. Johor Bahru Utara district police chief ACP Radin Ramlan Radin Taha said police received a report at about 7.33 pm from a witness who heard a baby crying from the location. “Further inspection found the baby girl wrapped in a towel and still with her umbilical cord,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: No diesel shortage, says domestic trade minister; delays at ports behind brief disruptions
items.summary: KUCHING, April 14 — The government has assured that diesel supply remains sufficient, but enforcement measures will continue to be strengthened to prevent leakages in the supply chain amid current global uncertainties. Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said several isolated disruptions had been reported, but these were due to logistical issues such as delays in deliveries to ports and depots, and not a shortage of supply. In this regard, the public is encouraged to lodge complaints via the official platforms of the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN) if any petrol stations face supply shortages,” he said.

items.title: Police bust Melaka scam call centre, arrest two Chinese nationals in fake job ring targeting US victims via Telegram
items.summary: MELAKA, April 14 — Two Chinese nationals have been arrested for allegedly being involved in a syndicate offering non-existent online part-time jobs, operating from a double-storey terrace house in Taman Puncak Bertam here yesterday. Melaka Tengah police chief ACP Christopher Patit said the two men, aged 28 and 38, who worked as customer service operators for the syndicate, were arrested during Op Frontier at about 10.30 am. He said the operation was carried out by the Intelligence/Operations division of the Melaka police contingent headquarters in collaboration with the Melaka Tengah district Commercial Crime Investigation Division.

items.title: Traffic cop injured in hit-and-run on Jalan Sultan Yahya Petra in KL; suspect held
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 14 — Police have arrested the driver suspected of being involved in a hit‑and‑run incident that injured a traffic police officer on Jalan Sultan Yahya Petra here on Friday. Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department (JSPT) chief ACP Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa confirmed that the suspect was detained yesterday to assist in the investigation into the incident. The driver then fled towards the Kuala Lumpur Police Training Centre (Pulapol).

items.title: MACC nabs federal agency CEO over RM1m bribe linked to squash court project
items.summary: MACC sources told Bernama that the male suspect, in his 40s, was detained at about 7.20 pm today at a shopping complex in Bukit Jalil. The arrest was made after the suspect was believed to have received RM100,000 in cash from a contractor. — Bernama

items.title: Malaysian writer Mohamed Nasser Mohamed shortlisted for Commonwealth short story prize
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 14 — A Malaysian writer has made the cut in this year’s Commonwealth Short Story Prize shortlist, with 25 writers from 14 countries selected from nearly 8,000 entries worldwide. Mohamed Nasser Mohamed is among seven writers shortlisted from Asia, with his story The Missing Half (Separuh Yang Hilang) — originally written in Malay and translated into English by Pauline Fan — highlighting the prize’s growing multilingual reach. The shortlisted writers comprise 11 men and 14 women, aged between 25 and 68.

items.title: As fuel costs rise, Malaysians urged to rethink spending, travel and daily habits
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 14 — Amid global economic uncertainty driven by geopolitical tensions and volatile crude oil prices, the key question is no longer only how effectively governments can manage the crisis, but how far the public is prepared to help limit its impact. Collective restraint would not only help the government contain rising subsidy costs but could also extend the availability of supply over a longer period. Fiscal strain and budget recalibration Rising subsidy costs remain a central concern, particularly if crude oil prices stay elevated while the government maintains the RON95 price at RM1.99 per litre despite mounting fiscal pressure.

items.title: WFH trims travel expenses yet raises home power bills, says expert
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 14 — For Suzana Abdullah, the flexibility of working from home (WFH) on a rotational basis implemented by her company has provided her with significant relief in terms of daily expenses. However, for Elthan Koo, 38, an IT consultant at a government-linked company in Cyberjaya, the WFH policy means higher electricity consumption at home. He said the policy is aimed at reducing fuel consumption and ensuring more efficient fuel usage, amid the current global energy crisis.

items.title: Not covered: Domestic Violence Act loophole leaves unmarried couples vulnerable
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 14 — Malaysia’s main law on domestic violence does not protect everyone facing abuse — leaving those in non-marital relationships without access to critical safeguards. The gap comes amid rising cases of domestic violence. This means survivors in non-marital relationships cannot access key legal safeguards designed to prevent further abuse, including Emergency Protection Orders (EPOs), Interim Protection Orders (IPOs), and Protection Orders (POs).

items.title: Asean should empower youth as decision-makers, not participants, says Kavita Sanglish
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 13 — Asean should shift from engaging youth as participants to empowering them as decision-makers to deliver more inclusive and future-ready regional policies, said youth advocate Kavita Sanglish. Kavita, who participated in the Asean Malaysia Youth Summit 2025 as both moderator and speaker, said the platform underscored a growing shift, with young people increasingly stepping forward to contribute at the regional level. Citing data from the World Health Organisation, she said nearly one in seven adolescents globally experiences a mental disorder, yet many do not receive adequate support.

items.title: Police to be deployed at Malaysia’s 151 border petrol stations in fuel smuggling crackdown, says DPM Fadillah
items.summary: KUCHING, April 13 — Police will be temporarily deployed at high-risk petrol stations along border areas as part of a crackdown on fuel smuggling, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof. He said the deployment covers 151 fuel stations flagged by the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN) for tighter monitoring. Also present were Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali and Sarawak State Secretary Datuk Amar Mohamad Abu Bakar Marzuki.

items.title: Local authorities told to roll out energy-saving measures immediately
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 13 — The Ministry of Housing and Local Government (KPKT), through the Department of Local Government (JKT), has urged all local authorities (PBTs) nationwide to strengthen the implementation of energy planning and conservation measures at the local level with immediate effect, said its minister Nga Kor Ming. “KPKT is committed to ensuring that all 156 local authorities take proactive and well-planned measures to optimise energy usage in buildings, facilities and daily operations, while ensuring that daily operations and counter services continue to run smoothly and efficiently to meet the needs of the public,” he said in a statement today. Nga said additional measures include increasing the use of green technology such as the installation of solar photovoltaic panels, energy-efficient LED lighting and smart building management systems in local authority facilities, as well as conducting regular energy audits and long-term planning to identify savings opportunities in buildings, street lighting, public markets, recreational parks and local infrastructure.

items.title: PKR to pick GE16 candidates from strong, active divisions, says Anwar
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, April 13 — PKR will only consider candidates from divisions with strong membership and grassroots machinery for the next general election (GE16). PKR president, Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, said the selection criteria would also depend on the level of programme activity carried out at the division level. Also present were PKR vice-president and Science, Technology and Innovation Minister Datuk Chang Lih Kang; PKR secretary-general and Deputy Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Dr Fuziah Salleh; and PKR information chief and Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil.

items.title: Home minister: 48 ex-armed forces personnel picked as first batch of Malaysia Border Guard
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 13 — A total of 48 veterans of the Malaysian Armed Forces (MAF) have been selected as the first group of the Malaysia Border Guard (MBG) under the Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (AKPS). Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said that the number is from 1,400 applicants to join the MBG. Saifuddin Nasution said the remaining MBG recruitment will be conducted within eight months to complete the quota of 220 members this year, and MBG members will operate according to AKPS assignments with an initial one-year contract.

items.title: Anwar urges swift, effective rollout of 13MP projects in Terengganu
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 13 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has called for projects under the 13th Malaysia Plan (13MP) in Terengganu to be implemented in a decisive, efficient and effective manner. “The Madani Government will ensure processes are streamlined, coordination strengthened and implementation expedited so that every initiative reaches the people without bureaucratic hurdles. Earlier, Anwar chaired the Kuala Terengganu district development meeting to review the progress of projects and implementation issues under 13MP.
